evidence_scope: bounded pure uBLK qid/tag lifecycle model; no uBLK daemon, ioctl, or product block I/O runtime behavior
runtime_claim_boundary: the runtime-ublk-completion-artifact class remains required before TideFS may strengthen this claim beyond model evidence
contract_boundary: legal read, write, flush, and discard submissions refine into tidefs-types-vfs-core block RequestEnvelope records and terminal TideCompletion records
checked_invariants: each qid/tag generation has at most one terminal TideFS completion
successful uBLK completion emits a matching TideCompletion for the current request token
abort emits one cancelled TideCompletion and rejects later uBLK completion commands
timeout emits one timed-out TideCompletion and stale completions after reissue fail closed
duplicate completion, stale generation, wrong qid, wrong tag, and completion after abort do not mutate the slot into a second terminal completion
